message:
feat(web): migrate 5 analysis overlays to chrome primitives (s4qx.3.5)

Migrated ComplexityOverlay, DotPlotOverlay, CodonBiasOverlay, PromoterOverlay,
and CRISPROverlay to use overlay chrome primitives for consistent structure.

Changes:
- Use OverlayStack for layout with consistent spacing
- Use OverlayDescription for title/info sections
- Use OverlayStatGrid + OverlayStatCard for metrics display
- Use OverlayLoadingState/OverlayEmptyState/OverlayErrorState for states
- Use OverlayLegend + OverlayLegendItem for chart legends
- Use OverlayToolbar for controls sections
- Replace inline color refs with CSS custom properties
- Extend OverlayStatCard label prop to ReactNode for InfoButton support

This completes the incremental migration of top analysis overlays to
demonstrate and validate the overlay chrome primitives pattern.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.3.2","title":"Audit + extend existing UI tokens (spacing, radius, overlay chrome)","description":"## Problem
We already have token infrastructure from prior polish work (see `phage_explorer-taos` / `phage_explorer-vntd` lineage), but new UI surfaces and one-off styling can reintroduce drift.

## Goal
Use **one canonical token set** for the web UI (no duplicate/near-duplicate CSS variables):
- spacing scale
- border radius scale
- overlay surface colors / borders
- elevation/shadows (subtle)
- timing/motion tokens (if not already standardized)

## Approach
- Audit existing token sources (e.g., `tokens.css`, theme variables, overlay-specific vars).
- Consolidate/rename only where it reduces confusion (avoid churn).
- Fill gaps needed for overlay primitives (header/footer/sections).

## Acceptance criteria
- Tokens are the *default* for new work: primitives consume tokens directly.
- No new “magic numbers” introduced for spacing/radius in overlay chrome.
- Token names are semantic and stable.

 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.3.3","title":"Create overlay chrome primitives (header/footer/sections)","description":"## Goal
Make overlays feel consistent and premium by standardizing their chrome.

## Scope
- Header: title, subtitle/description, close affordance, shortcut hint
- Body: consistent padding and section spacing
- Footer: primary/secondary actions when needed

## Acceptance criteria
- At least 2 overlays are migrated to use the primitives.
- No visual regression in overlay stacking and mobile bottom sheet variant.

 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.3.4","title":"Migrate DataLoadingOverlay + base overlay chrome to primitives","description":"## Goal
Apply the design system to the most visible “first impression” surface: loading.

## Scope
- DataLoadingOverlay (and any shared loading overlay UI)
- Base overlay container/chrome where appropriate (padding, header/footer spacing)

## Out of scope
- Help overlay migration is tracked separately so we don’t couple visual refactors to help-content work.

## Acceptance criteria
- Loading overlay uses standardized tokens/primitives.
- No leftover inline-style layout hacks.
- Loading/empty/error primitives are reused consistently.

- represent different layouts (charts, tables, long text)

## Acceptance criteria
- Migrations are incremental and reviewed (avoid bulk refactor).
- Visual consistency improves without breaking interactions.

 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.3.6","title":"Document design-system conventions for overlays","description":"## Goal
Make future UI work naturally consistent by documenting conventions.

## Contents
- token usage
- overlay chrome primitives
- spacing/typography guidelines
- do/don’t examples (avoid inline style drift)

## Acceptance criteria
- A short doc exists in the web package or root docs that future contributors can follow.

 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.3.7","title":"Help overlay: migrate to overlay primitives (premium layout + typography)","description":"## Goal
Bring the Help overlay up to premium standards visually once its content and registry wiring are stable.

## Scope
- Apply overlay chrome primitives (header/footer/sections)
- Premium typography hierarchy (title/subtitle/body/captions)
- Clean, scannable layout for shortcut tables (alignment, spacing, grouping)

## Acceptance criteria
- Help overlay reads cleanly and matches the rest of the overlay system.
- No hardcoded styling drift; uses tokens/primitives.
- No contradictions with ActionRegistry-driven content.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-05T20:22:18.292177724-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T20:22:18.292177724-06:00","labels":["design-system","help","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.3.7","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-05T20:22:18.294080229-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.3.7","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-05T20:22:30.096429322-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.3.7","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-05T20:22:35.336245811-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.4","title":"Typography + hierarchy: sans UI, mono data","description":"## Problem
Global monospace typography in the web UI makes the product feel like a developer tool and reduces readability for long-form UI text.

## Goal
Adopt a premium typography baseline:
- Sans-serif for UI chrome, navigation, headings, and descriptive text
- Monospace reserved for sequences, coordinates, identifiers, and code-like data

## Approach
- Update typography CSS baseline and apply targeted mono classes to data surfaces.
- Tune line-height and font-weight for readability.
- Ensure consistency across:
  - overlay headers
  - tables/lists
  - search results
  - tooltips

## Acceptance criteria
- UI chrome uses sans-serif by default.
- Sequences and “data/terminal” areas remain monospace.
- No layout regressions in sequence grid.

## Touchpoints
- `packages/web/src/styles/typography.css`
- sequence grid components

 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.5.5","title":"Tune default look: quiet premium (less gimmick, more clarity)","description":"## Goal
Adjust default visual presentation to feel premium and analysis-first.

## Considerations
- Keep the “phage hacker” aesthetic available, but not as the first impression.
- Prefer subtle depth, clean typography, and consistent spacing.

## Acceptance criteria
- First-time load feels modern and premium.
- Users can still opt into more stylized FX.

 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.5.6","title":"Regression coverage: mobile scroll + FX stability","description":"## Goal
Protect against reintroducing mobile scroll flicker and FX-related repaint issues.

## Notes
A prior fix exists (see `phage_explorer-q5ru`, closed 2026-01-05). This task ensures the fix stays intact as we adjust defaults.

## Acceptance criteria
- E2E tests cover core mobile scroll interactions with default settings.
- No console errors.

 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6","title":"Loading/empty/error UX polish (perceived performance + clarity)","description":"## Problem
Perceived performance is heavily determined by:
- loading skeletons and progressive rendering
- clear empty states (what to do next)
- error states that explain and recover

Even with fast underlying compute, blank screens or vague messages make the product feel slow and unfinished.

## Goal
Make every major surface feel “alive” and self-explanatory:
- consistent loading skeletons
- actionable empty states
- friendly error presentation and recovery

## Approach
- Standardize a small set of components/patterns:
  - `LoadingState` / skeleton
  - `EmptyState` with next actions
  - `ErrorState` with retry and details (as appropriate)
- Audit major surfaces (search, overlays, DB load, 3D load) and retrofit.

## Acceptance criteria
- No major surface shows a blank/unstyled loading state.
- Empty states suggest next step.
- Errors provide a retry path when possible.

## Touchpoints
- overlay loading components
- data fetching / DB init
- 3D structure loading UI
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-05T19:47:33.341929298-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T19:47:33.341929298-06:00","labels":["loading","polish","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-05T19:47:33.343789347-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6.1","title":"Standardize LoadingState / EmptyState / ErrorState primitives","description":"## Goal
Create consistent primitives for loading, empty, and error states.

## Requirements
- Visual consistency with design tokens (spacing, typography).
- Clear next actions:
  - loading: show progress when possible
  - empty: show what to do next
  - error: show retry + minimal details; optionally expandable diagnostics

## Acceptance criteria
- At least one major surface is migrated to these primitives.

 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6.2","title":"Audit + retrofit loading/empty/error states across major surfaces (excluding DB init + 3D)","description":"## Goal
Systematically eliminate “blank screen” and vague states in major web surfaces.

## Scope
This task covers major surfaces **other than**:
- DB initialization UX (tracked separately in `phage_explorer-s4qx.6.3`)
- 3D viewer loading UX (tracked separately in `phage_explorer-s4qx.6.4`)

## Targets (examples)
- search results + search overlay worker init
- analysis overlays (compute + data fetch)
- phage selection transitions
- error presentation for common recoverable failures

## Acceptance criteria
- Each target has a clear loading/empty/error state.
- Copy is helpful and actionable.
- Uses the standardized primitives from `phage_explorer-s4qx.6.1`.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-05T19:54:29.052483176-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T20:12:14.201129273-06:00","labels":["loading","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6.2","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-05T19:54:29.054235483-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6.2","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-05T20:02:06.824098157-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6.3","title":"DB initialization UX: progress, retry, offline clarity","description":"## Goal
Make the initial DB load feel reliable and transparent.

## Requirements
- Clear “what is happening” and expected wait time (when possible).
- If load fails, show:
  - friendly explanation
  - retry button
  - lightweight diagnostics (e.g., asset fetch failed)

## Acceptance criteria
- No silent failure.
- Users can recover without refreshing the whole page.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-05T19:54:40.119216249-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T19:54:40.119216249-06:00","labels":["db","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6.3","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-05T19:54:40.121176083-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6.3","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-05T20:02:12.061124966-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6.4","title":"3D viewer loading UX: progressive states + recoverable errors","description":"## Goal
Make 3D structure loading feel fast and trustworthy.

## Ideas
- skeleton for viewer
- progressive messaging (fetch → parse → compute bonds → render)
- clear error handling with retry

## Acceptance criteria
- Users understand what stage loading is at.
- Failures are actionable.
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-05T19:54:50.08801132-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T19:54:50.08801132-06:00","labels":["3d","loading","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6.4","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-05T19:54:50.089836081-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6.4","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-s4qx.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-05T20:02:17.299959555-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
